commands.review_summary:
This PR makes Codex app-server bootstrap injection reporting tolerate bootstrap files without `name`, derive display labels from `path`, preserve path-first lookup, and add a hook-supplied path-only regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: a hook-preserved path-only bootstrap file can reach `buildCodexBootstrapInjectionStats`, and current main can call the basename helper with an undefined `file.name`. I did not execute the repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-owned real behavior proof or maintainer override, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): guard path-only bootstrap files [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Codex app-server startup/reporting crash when bootstrap context files are supplied with `path` and `content` but no `name`.

The patch keeps path-based lookup first, treats the filename as optional, and derives the report display name from the path when needed.

## Why

OpenClaw can carry context files shaped like:

```json
{ "path": "/workspace/AGENTS.md", "content": "..." }
```

The Codex system prompt report path later assumed `name` was always present and could call `.trim()` through the basename helper with `undefined`.

## What changed

- Guard optional bootstrap file names in the Codex injection stats path.
- Keep lookup by full `path` before trying filename or basename lookup.
- Derive a stable display name from `path` when `name` is absent.
- Add a regression test through the real `runCodexAppServerAttempt()` harness with a hook-supplied path-only bootstrap file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server startup/reporting could crash while building the system prompt report when a bootstrap context file had `path` and `content` but no `name`; the failing path was `runCodexAppServerAttempt -> buildCodexSystemPromptReport -> buildCodexBootstrapInjectionStats -> readCodexIndexedContextFileContent -> getCodexContextFileBasename`.

Real environment tested: isolated Linux OpenClaw testserver clone of `openclaw/openclaw` at current main `b58572e283`, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0, no live provider credentials or secrets. The proof exercised the real Codex app-server attempt harness with an `agent:bootstrap` hook that supplied a path-only bootstrap file.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the patch to the isolated OpenClaw checkout, installed with the locked pnpm workspace, then ran:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "reports hook-supplied bootstrap files"
```

Before evidence: the same path-only bootstrap scenario on current main reproduced the crash:

```text
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
normalizeCodexContextFilePath
getCodexContextFileBasename
readCodexIndexedContextFileContent
buildCodexBootstrapInjectionStats
buildCodexSystemPromptReport
runCodexAppServerAttempt
```

After-fix evidence: copied live terminal output from the isolated OpenClaw testserver showed the app-server attempt completed and reported the hook-supplied path-only file as `SOUL.md` instead of crashing:

```text
RUN v4.1.6 /openclaw-pr-tests/runs/2026-05-21-codex-context-file-name-guard/repo

✓ extensions ../../extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
  ✓ reports hook-supplied bootstrap files that only expose path and content

Observed report assertion from the completed app-server run:
injectedWorkspaceFiles[0].name = "SOUL.md"
injectedWorkspaceFiles[0].truncated = false

Test Files 1 passed
Tests 1 passed | 185 skipped
```

Observed result after fix: the Codex app-server attempt no longer calls the basename helper with `undefined`; the system prompt report keeps the full `path` lookup and emits a stable display name for the path-only bootstrap file.

What was not tested: no live provider request, OAuth flow, routing, or config-shape behavior was changed or tested.

## Additional validation

Before, the new regression test reproduced the exact crash on current main:

```text
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
normalizeCodexContextFilePath
getCodexContextFileBasename
readCodexIndexedContextFileContent
buildCodexBootstrapInjectionStats
buildCodexSystemPromptReport
runCodexAppServerAttempt
```

After the patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "reports hook-supplied bootstrap files"
1 passed | 185 skipped
```

Additional validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.test.ts src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.buildbootstrapcontextfiles.test.ts
6 files / 301 tests passed

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
passed

git diff --check
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
0 warnings / 0 errors

pnpm check:changed
passed
```

## Notes

No provider, OAuth, routing, config-shape, or fallback behavior is changed.
